name: United States Antimony Corporat

profile.description: United States Antimony Corporation produces and sells antimony, zeolite, and precious metals in the United States and Canada. It operates in two segments: Antimony and Zeolite. The company offers antimony trioxide that is primarily used in conjunction with a halogen to form a synergistic flame retardant system for plastics, rubber, fiberglass, textile goods, paints, coatings, and paper, as well as color fastener in paints and as a phosphorescent agent in fluorescent light bulbs; antimony metal for use in bearings, storage batteries, and ordnance; and antimony trisulfide used as a primer in ammunition. The company also offers coarse and fine zeolite for soil amendment and fertilizer, water filtration, mine underground ventilation, sewage treatment, nuclear waste and other environmental cleanup, odor control, gas separation, and animal nutrition applications, as well as catalysts, petroleum refining, concrete, solar energy and heat exchange, desiccants, pellet binding, horse and kitty litter, floor cleaner, traction control, ammonia removal from mining waste, and carriers for insecticides, pesticides and herbicides. In addition, it recovers unrefined and refined gold and silver. United States Antimony Corporation was founded in 1968 and is headquartered in Dallas, Texas.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: Track Record (1/3): UAMY has secured a transformative Defense Logistics Agency (DLA) contract for antimony ingot deliveries, potentially scaling from $6.8M Q1 revenue to $125M full-year guided revenue [1]. AI Deployment (0/2): No AI deployment. Market Share (1/2): Sole significant US antimony producer, creating a unique strategic position for defense and critical minerals supply chains. Pipeline (1/2): DLA contract expected to deliver $75-95M in government revenue, with Montana mining operations ramping [1]. R&D Intensity (1/1): Processing technology investment for antimony separation and purification.

inspiration.label: WEAK
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): US antimony independence for national defense is a specific, genuine purpose. Community Impact (0/2): No specific programs. Employee Retention (0/2): Small company with no data. Insider Buying (1/2): Management has equity stakes.

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored. Q1 2026 revenue of $6.8M was essentially flat YoY, with a net loss of $11.3M [1]. Full-year guidance of $125M represents potential 18x revenue growth if DLA deliveries materialize on schedule. The extreme revenue backend-loading creates high execution risk. Gross margins did not yet benefit from Montana mining or DLA deliveries, both expected in remaining quarters [1].

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): Antimony mining and processing is quintessentially physical — extracting, smelting, and refining critical minerals requires specialized facilities and expertise that AI cannot replicate [1]. Antimony is a critical mineral for defense (ammunition, flame retardants, batteries) with no AI substitution risk. Rising technology demand may increase antimony consumption. AI Opportunity (1/1): Antimony is used in semiconductor manufacturing and advanced batteries, creating indirect AI-driven demand.

infinite_game.summary: UAMY is a small, mission-driven company focused on US antimony independence for defense applications. The DLA contract validates the purpose, but the company is too small and early-stage to demonstrate mature infinite game characteristics.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (1/3): US critical minerals independence is meaningful but primarily government-contract-driven.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): Acknowledges Chinese antimony dominance as competitive pressure driving US domestic production.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): Persisted through years of minimal revenue to secure the DLA contract.

investment_thesis: UAMY is the sole significant US antimony producer with a transformative DLA contract guiding $125M in full-year 2026 revenue ($75-95M from government deliveries) [1]. If execution materializes, this represents 18x revenue growth from Q1's $6.8M annualized run rate. Antimony is a critical defense mineral with no domestic alternative, creating a unique strategic moat. At $8.98/share, the market is pricing in execution risk.
thesis_continued: Q1 2026 revenue of $6.8M with a $11.3M net loss demonstrates that the transformative growth is entirely forward-looking and unproven [1]. The $125M guidance is 18x the current run rate — any DLA delivery delays, production problems at the Montana mine, or contract modifications could dramatically reduce actual revenue. The company has a history of missing ambitious targets. Cash burn continues at an unsustainable rate.
key_catalysts: DLA antimony ingot deliveries commencing in remaining 2026 quarters, validating the $75-95M government revenue guidance
Montana mining operations ramping production and contributing to gross margins for the first time
Critical minerals legislation expanding government procurement of domestic antimony beyond the current DLA contract
key_risks: DLA contract delivery delays or production challenges at Montana mine causing full-year revenue to fall dramatically short of $125M guidance
Cash burn from $11.3M quarterly losses depleting resources before government revenue materializes
Antimony pricing volatility if China increases export quotas or global demand shifts
sources: United States Antimony Q1 2026 Results and Full-Year Guidance, May 2026
Yahoo Finance — UAMY Key Statistics, accessed June 2026
